criteria_checked.criterion_ref: R2-B3 fold (disarm-window mechanism named) — MACHINE-VERIFIED EXECUTABLE
criteria_checked.note: Mechanism named in Finding lines 87-93, Proposed Changes 215-217, T003 345-351, assertions row 379. Precedent cite is ACCURATE: .harness/evidence/dmc-stray-hygiene-build-20260708.md:51-70 records disarm windows #1/#2 with the identical sequence, including the cmd_resume-does-not-rewrite-the-pointer fact that necessitates the printf re-arm. Empirical Ring-0 classification (this review): the disarm command 'rm -f .harness/runs/current-run-id' classifies ALLOW rc=0 (BASH-L1-NO-WRITE) so the window has no chicken-and-egg under an armed run; the printf restore classifies OUT-OF-SCOPE rc=4 IF armed, but executes at a moment the repo is UNARMED (pointer absent, Block D not invoked) — so the ORDER is load-bearing and the plan/precedent order satisfies it.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: Option-B consent + spike-stop constraints (unchanged from r2 review)
criteria_checked.note: Preserved verbatim-or-stronger: user-run, never DMC-initiated, own gate + own scope (D2), bypass flag forbidden, Local session, honest abort rule (isolation is the safety property), clone trust removable, real-repo trust independent/optional, no promotion in any outcome (D5).

During this three-round review the Claude-side floors denied the critic's own commands three times (r2 verdict heredoc carrying floor-matching literals; r3 scan batch carrying a space-preceded env-probe word) and the review honored each denial by rephrasing, never weakening. Together with the two Ring-0 classifier runs, this is live evidence the floor tier the runbook relies on for the ORCHESTRATOR side behaves as documented — worth citing in cycle evidence alongside the hygiene precedent's identical incident log (dmc-stray-hygiene-build-20260708.md:72-75).
